events.text: Patient is a 55-year-old Caucasian female with a personal history of dysplastic nevi and family history of melanoma in two first-degree relatives, presenting with a new pigmented lesion on her right thigh.

At age 55, patient was diagnosed with superficial spreading melanoma (ICD-O morphology 8743/3) of the skin of the lower limb (ICD-O topography C44.7), staged as Stage IIID (T4bN3bM0) with extensive regional lymph node involvement.

At age 55, excisional biopsy of the right thigh lesion revealed superficial spreading melanoma with Breslow thickness of 5.6 mm, Clark level V, ulceration present, mitotic rate 10/mm2, microsatellites present, regression absent, lymphovascular invasion identified.
At age 55, wide local excision with 2 cm margins of the right thigh melanoma was performed with primary closure and all margins were confirmed negative.
At age 55, sentinel lymph node biopsy of the right inguinal region showed 3 of 4 sentinel nodes positive for metastatic melanoma, largest deposit measuring 12 mm with extracapsular extension.
At age 55, completion right inguinal and iliac lymph node dissection was performed yielding 16 nodes, of which 4 additional nodes were positive (total 7 of 20 nodes positive).

At age 55, completion lymph node dissection pathology showed metastatic melanoma in 7 of 20 total nodes with extracapsular extension in 3 nodes, largest deposit 12 mm, matted nodes present in right inguinal basin.

At age 55, comprehensive genomic profiling revealed BRAF V600E mutation (allele frequency 52%), NRAS wild-type, TERT promoter mutation (C228T), CDKN2A homozygous deletion, BAP1 loss-of-function mutation, and tumor mutational burden of 16 mutations per megabase.
At age 55, staging PET/CT post-operatively showed no distant metastatic disease but FDG-avid right inguinal region consistent with residual post-surgical inflammation.
At age 55, MRI brain with contrast was negative for intracranial metastatic disease.

At age 55, LDH was 198 U/L within normal limits, and multidisciplinary tumor board recommended adjuvant combination BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy given the extensive nodal disease burden and BRAF V600E status.
At age 55, adjuvant dabrafenib 150 mg twice daily plus trametinib 2 mg daily was initiated for resected Stage IIID melanoma.

At age 55, patient developed grade 3 pyrexia (temperature 40.1 C) with rigors and hypotension within 10 days of starting dabrafenib/trametinib, requiring emergency department visit, IV fluids, and dabrafenib dose reduction to 75 mg twice daily.
At age 55, despite dose reduction, patient experienced recurrent grade 2 pyrexia episodes, and dabrafenib was further managed with prophylactic corticosteroid protocol with improved tolerability.
At age 56, 6-month surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis showed no evidence of recurrent disease and resolved post-surgical changes.
At age 56, 12-month surveillance PET/CT was negative for recurrent or metastatic disease.
At age 56, patient completed 12 months of adjuvant dabrafenib/trametinib at reduced dabrafenib dose, tolerating with manageable pyrexia on prophylactic corticosteroid protocol, LDH normal at 175 U/L.
At age 56, post-treatment MRI brain surveillance was negative for intracranial metastatic disease.
At age 57, 6-month post-treatment surveillance CT showed new 1.5 cm right lower lobe pulmonary nodule and 1.0 cm left hepatic lobe lesion suspicious for metastatic disease.
At age 57, following CT-guided biopsy of the pulmonary nodule confirming metastatic melanoma, patient was restaged as Stage IV (M1c) with pulmonary and hepatic metastases, LDH elevated at 265 U/L.
At age 57, CT-guided core biopsy of the right lower lobe nodule showed metastatic melanoma positive for S100, SOX10, and BRAF V600E on reflex molecular testing.
At age 57, combination ipilimumab 3 mg/kg plus nivolumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks was initiated for metastatic melanoma given prior exposure to BRAF/MEK inhibitor therapy in the adjuvant setting.
At age 57, after cycle 2 of ipilimumab/nivolumab, patient developed grade 2 immune-related diarrhea (5-6 stools/day) managed with oral budesonide and loperamide without treatment interruption.
At age 57, restaging CT at 12 weeks showed partial response with 35% reduction in pulmonary nodule and stable hepatic lesion.
At age 57, LDH decreased to 218 U/L on ipilimumab/nivolumab and patient continued with nivolumab maintenance after completing 4 cycles of combination therapy.

scenario_blurb: Stage III/IV melanoma with BRAF V600E mutation. Treated with combination BRAF/MEK inhibitors (dabrafenib/trametinib) and/or checkpoint immunotherapy (nivolumab plus ipilimumab, pembrolizumab). Some with brain metastases. Include LDH levels.
